definition_en: ===Proper noun=== # ====Statistics==== According to data collected by Forebears in 2014, Moyo was the most common surname in Zimbabwe (327 457 persons = 2.5 percent of the population).
rebellion, revolution; a war fought against European colonists
forest; fruit of the mutondo tree; florescence
sit, be seated; stay, live (somewhere); stay away from
bottle; piece of glass used to scrape
ox, a young or untrained one
now, at present; then, so then
a kind of round dish used for relish
heart (used in anatomy to refer to the muscular organ which pumps blood throughout the body, as well as in "metaphorical" terms in reference to emotional or romantic experiences)
A member of the Rozvi tribe, the ruling class of the .
spirit elder of the family; soul of a dead person
a type of very large drum
Milky Way (in Chingova); Galaxy (possible use in Standard Shona)
be quiet, be silent; be peaceful
woo, court; gain favor with someone; have sex
to see; to think; to acquire
# to be seen, to be visible
to take warmth from a fire.
to act secretly; # to keep oneself hidden
to count; # to compute; to read
night (in two Shona languages, Karanga and Ndau language).; nighttime
to know; to be acquainted with
===Etymology=== From , plus augment. Originally the pronominal and verbal concord, it displaced the older Bantu noun prefix . The tone was lowered by analogy with other noun prefixes. ===Prefix=== #
